name: Frederick Law Olmsted
practice: Public park design, democratic landscape, nature as public health infrastructure
approach: Parks are not decoration — they are public health infrastructure. Central Park was designed so that a factory worker could experience the psychological restoration of nature without leaving the city. Democratic access to landscape beauty.
body_of_work: Central Park, Prospect Park, Emerald Necklace (Boston), US Capitol grounds, Biltmore Estate. Designed the first public park system in America. Also reported on slavery in the antebellum South as a journalist.
why_instructive: He invented the idea that designed landscape is public health infrastructure — exactly what sphere layer 3 requires. His parks are spheres that have been activating for 150 years.

dome_relevance.4: Parks as public health infrastructure — attention restoration, stress reduction
dome_relevance.8: Community — parks as democratic gathering space
dome_relevance.9: Environment — nature brought into the city

sphere_relevance.1: Parcel design — topography, circulation, spatial sequence
sphere_relevance.3: Environmental — water management, microclimate, ecology within the city
sphere_relevance.5: Social — democratic access regardless of class
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — parks that have been activating for 150+ years
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the park experience IS the activation
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — living systems that grow stronger over decades
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — Central Park activated all of Manhattan's development
